# 🕸️ Tech / Startups GraphRAG

A GraphRAG system over a Neo4j knowledge graph of startups, founders,
investors, products, and awards — with a FastAPI backend, a fixed
library of hand-written Cypher queries, a Groq-powered answer-synthesis
step, and an interactive Streamlit frontend. Built to deploy on Railway.

**Cypher is never generated by an LLM in this project.** A
natural-language question is matched, by a rule-based matcher, against
one of ~18 fixed, parameterized, read-only Cypher templates. That fixed
query — never anything a model wrote — is what runs against Neo4j. The
LLM's only job is turning the rows that come back into a natural-language
sentence. See [`CYPHER.md`](CYPHER.md) for the complete, documented
catalog of every query the API can ever run.

```
question in natural language
        │
        ▼
Rule-based matcher ── selects ONE fixed Cypher template (app/core/cypher_library.py)
        │                        no template matched?
        │                               │
        ▼                               ▼
Cypher safety validator          Fulltext fallback search
(blocks writes/admin ops,                │
 requires RETURN + LIMIT)                │
        │                                │
        ▼                                │
Neo4j (READ transaction only) ── zero rows? ──┘
        │
        ▼
Groq LLM ── synthesizes a grounded, no-hallucination answer from the rows
        │
        ▼
FastAPI /answer  →  Streamlit chat UI (answer + matched query + raw rows + graph view)
```

## Project layout

```
.github/workflows/ci.yml     Lint + data pipeline + test suite on every push
app/
  main.py                    FastAPI app entrypoint
  config.py                  Env-driven settings (pydantic-settings)
  schemas.py                 Request/response models
  db/neo4j_client.py         Pooled Neo4j driver, enforced READ transactions
  core/
    cypher_library.py        The fixed Cypher template library + question matcher (no LLM)
    prompts.py                Answer-synthesis system prompt (the only LLM call in the app)
    llm.py                     Groq chat completion wrapper (retried)
    graph_rag.py                Orchestration: match → validate → run → fallback → synthesize
  api/routes.py               GET /health, POST /answer
  frontend/streamlit_app.py   Interactive chat UI, matched-query/results/graph tabs
data/
  raw/                        Drop your own source CSVs here (see data/raw/README.md) — gitignored/empty by default
  processed/                  Cleaned CSVs + cleaning_report.md (generated, gitignored)
  schema/ontology.md          Ontology design write-up
scripts/
  clean_data.py                Real ETL: dedup, normalize, resolve dangling refs
  load_neo4j.py                 Idempotent MERGE-based loader + constraints/indexes
tests/                        pytest suite (health, answer, Cypher library, Cypher safety, cleaning logic)
CYPHER.md                     Full catalog of every Cypher query the app can run
.env.example                  All required environment variables
railway.json / Procfile       Railway deploy config (API + Streamlit as 2 services)
Makefile                      One-line commands for the full workflow
ruff.toml / pytest.ini        Lint/test config
```

## Why fixed Cypher instead of LLM-generated Cypher

- **No injection surface.** Every query that can ever run against Neo4j
  is a string written by a human and checked into `cypher_library.py`.
  There's no path from "arbitrary user text" to "arbitrary Cypher" —
  only fixed templates with parameters bound through the driver.
- **Deterministic.** The same question always maps to the same query.
  Nothing depends on a model's mood, a prompt-injection attempt buried
  in the question, or an LLM provider being up.
- **Fast and free to match.** Regex matching is microseconds and costs
  nothing; the only LLM call left is the answer-synthesis step.
- **Fully testable offline.** `tests/test_cypher_library.py` exercises
  the matcher and validates every template with no network calls at all.

The tradeoff is coverage: a question has to resemble one of the ~18
patterns in `CYPHER.md` (or contain a recognizable entity name for the
fulltext fallback) to get a real answer, rather than being able to
handle arbitrary phrasing. Extending coverage means adding a new
template to `cypher_library.py` (and its entry in `CYPHER.md`) — not
tuning a prompt.

## Ontology

See [`data/schema/ontology.md`](data/schema/ontology.md) for the full write-up.
Short version:

- **Nodes:** `Person`, `Company`, `Investor`, `Product`, `Award`
- **Relationships:** `(Person)-[FOUNDED]->(Company)`, `(Person)-[WORKS_AT]->(Company)`,
  `(Investor)-[INVESTED_IN]->(Company)`, `(Company)-[ACQUIRED]->(Company)`,
  `(Company)-[DEVELOPS]->(Product)`, `(Person|Company)-[WON]->(Award)`

## Data quality handling

`data/raw/` ships empty (see [`data/raw/README.md`](data/raw/README.md) for
the exact filenames/columns expected) — drop your own source-system
exports in there. `scripts/clean_data.py` is built to fix, rather than
hide, the kind of messiness real exports have:

| Issue in raw data | Fix |
|---|---|
| Exact duplicate rows | Dropped after normalization |
| Inconsistent casing (`founder` vs `Founder`, `ai` vs `AI`, `elena rossi`/`novapay`) | Canonical-name index resolves every relationship endpoint to one display name |
| Mixed boolean formats (`yes`/`Y`/`TRUE`/`FALSE`) | Parsed to real booleans |
| Mixed numeric formats (`2016.0`) | Coerced to nullable ints/floats |
| Missing values (hometown, valuation, founded year, launch year) | Kept as `NULL`, never guessed |
| Dangling references (a relationship row pointing at an entity missing from its own node file) | Loaded as an explicit placeholder node tagged `data_quality: "placeholder"` so the edge isn't silently dropped, and the answer-synthesis prompt is instructed to flag those as lower-confidence |

Run `python scripts/clean_data.py` and read `data/processed/cleaning_report.md`
for the full, itemized before/after log.

## Local setup

```bash
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
make install

cp .env.example .env
# fill in NEO4J_URI / NEO4J_USERNAME / NEO4J_PASSWORD and GROQ_API_KEY

# add your own CSVs to data/raw/ first — see data/raw/README.md
make clean          # data/raw -> data/processed
make load-graph      # data/processed -> Neo4j (idempotent, safe to re-run)
make test             # pytest, mocks Neo4j/Groq so no live services needed

make api               # FastAPI on :8000  (in one terminal)
make frontend            # Streamlit on :8501 (in another terminal)
```

Open `http://localhost:8501`. The sidebar's health indicator confirms both
Neo4j and Groq are reachable before you start asking questions. See the
sidebar's example questions, or [`CYPHER.md`](CYPHER.md), for phrasing that's
guaranteed to match a template.

## API

### `GET /health`
Reports Neo4j connectivity and whether `GROQ_API_KEY` is configured.

```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "components": [
    {"name": "neo4j", "status": "ok", "detail": "connected"},
    {"name": "groq", "status": "ok", "detail": "GROQ_API_KEY is set"}
  ],
  "version": "1.0.0"
}
```

### `POST /answer`
```json
{ "question": "Who founded NovaPay?", "top_k": 25, "include_subgraph": true }
```

```json
{
  "question": "Who founded NovaPay?",
  "answer": "NovaPay was founded by Elena Rossi in 2016.",
  "cypher": "MATCH (p:Person)-[r:FOUNDED]->(c:Company) WHERE toLower(c.name) CONTAINS toLower($name) RETURN p.name AS founder, c.name AS company, r.year AS year ORDER BY r.year LIMIT $limit",
  "cypher_params": {"name": "NovaPay", "limit": 25},
  "cypher_valid": true,
  "template_id": "founders_of_company",
  "template_description": "The founder(s) of a given company, and founding year.",
  "row_count": 1,
  "results": [{"founder": "Elena Rossi", "company": "NovaPay", "year": 2016}],
  "used_fallback_search": false,
  "warnings": [],
  "latency_ms": 480,
  "model_used": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile"
}
```

`template_id` / `template_description` identify exactly which entry in
[`CYPHER.md`](CYPHER.md) answered the question, so any client can show the
user precisely which fixed query ran — never "the model generated this."

## Cypher safety

Even though Cypher is never LLM-generated, every resolved query is still
treated as something to double-check before it runs, in layers:

1. A regex-based validator (`app/core/graph_rag.py::validate_cypher`) rejects
   any query containing `CREATE`, `MERGE`, `SET`, `DELETE`, `DETACH`,
   `REMOVE`, `DROP`, `LOAD CSV`, mutating APOC procedures, or admin commands,
   and rejects queries with no `RETURN` or no `LIMIT`.
2. Every query is additionally forced to run inside an explicit Neo4j **READ**
   transaction (`session.execute_read`), which Neo4j itself will refuse to
   run a write against — defense in depth, not just template trust.
3. `LIMIT $limit` is always present in every template and capped
   server-side to `MAX_CYPHER_ROWS` regardless of what the client requested.
4. If the matched query runs but returns zero rows (or no template
   matched at all), a fulltext fallback search finds the closest-matching
   entity names instead of just failing.
5. The one piece of dynamic query-text assembly in the whole library —
   the comparison operator in `companies_by_sector_and_valuation`
   (`over`/`under`/`at least`/...) — is chosen from a fixed 10-entry
   whitelist, never taken verbatim from user text. See `CYPHER.md` for details.

## Deploying to Railway

This repo runs as **two Railway services** from the same GitHub repo:

1. **API service** — uses `railway.json` as-is (`uvicorn app.main:app`).
   Set `NEO4J_URI`, `NEO4J_USERNAME`, `NEO4J_PASSWORD`, `GROQ_API_KEY` (and
   optionally `NEO4J_DATABASE`, `CORS_ORIGINS`) as Railway environment
   variables.
2. **Frontend service** — same repo, override the start command to:
   `streamlit run app/frontend/streamlit_app.py --server.port $PORT --server.address 0.0.0.0 --server.headless true`
   (this is also predefined as the `frontend` process in the `Procfile`).
   Set `BACKEND_URL` to the API service's public Railway URL.
3. **Neo4j** — use [Neo4j AuraDB](https://neo4j.com/cloud/platform/aura-graph-database/)
   (free tier works fine for this dataset) or Railway's Neo4j template; point
   `NEO4J_URI` at it.
4. After both services are up, run `python scripts/load_neo4j.py` once
   (locally, pointed at the Aura instance via `.env`, or as a one-off Railway
   run) to populate the graph.

## Extending the query library

To support a new question shape:

1. Add a `QueryTemplate` to `app/core/cypher_library.py`: an `id`, a
   short `description`, one or more `patterns` (compiled regexes with
   named capture groups), the fixed `cypher` string (must include
   `RETURN` and `LIMIT $limit`), and a `build_params` function that
   turns a regex match into a parameter dict.
2. Register it with `_register(...)`, placing it before any more-general
   template it could otherwise be shadowed by (matching is first-match-wins
   in registration order).
3. Add the matching entry to [`CYPHER.md`](CYPHER.md).
4. Add a test case to `tests/test_cypher_library.py`.

## Testing & linting

```bash
make test    # pytest — health, answer endpoint, Cypher template library, Cypher safety, cleaning logic
make lint     # ruff check
make format    # ruff format
```

CI (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) runs lint, the cleaning pipeline, and the
full test suite on every push/PR — no live Neo4j/Groq needed since the
backend tests mock the Neo4j client and stub the LLM calls, and Cypher
matching is pure regex with no external dependency at all.
